curriculum.subject: Chemistry
curriculum.overallDescription: 19 units, 19 chapters, and 11 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Understand nature of matter, properties, measurement, uncertainty, laws of chemical combination, Dalton's atomic theory
Calculate atomic/molecular masses, mole concept, molar mass, percentage composition, stoichiometry
Trace discoveries of sub-atomic particles and development leading to Bohr's model
Apply modern periodic law and interpret the present form of Periodic Table
Apply Kossel-Lewis approach and understand Ionic/Electrovalent bonds
Analyze applications of thermodynamics and measurement of ∆U and ∆H
Apply Law of Chemical Equilibrium and interpret relationship between K, Q, and Gibbs Energy
Classify redox reactions based on classical ideas and electron transfer mechanisms
Apply structural representations and classification of organic compounds
Differentiate between types of hydrocarbons based on structure and bonding

topics: Mole Concept, Molar Masses, Percentage Composition
Discovery and Models of the Atom
Sub-atomic Particles, Bohr's Model for Hydrogen

Class record and viva

practicals.details: Chemical Equilibrium experiment (Class XI). Study effect of concentration change.

Determination of strength of NaOH by titration against Oxalic acid
Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds

Preparation of lyophobic sol (Al(OH)3, Fe(OH)3, As2S3)

Study role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing emulsion
practicals.details: Surface Chemistry experiment (Class XII). Study effect on different oils.

Study reaction rates: Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide or Clock Reaction

Enthalpy of neutralization of strong acid and base
Determination of enthalpy change during interaction between Acetone and Chloroform
practicals.details: Thermochemistry experiment (Class XII). Hydrogen bond formation.

practicals.title: Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn2+ || Cu2+/Cu with concentration change
practicals.details: Electrochemistry experiment (Class XII). Study effect of electrolyte concentration.

practicals.title: Separation of pigments from leaves/flowers by paper chromatography and Rf determination
Preparation of double salt: Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum
practicals.details: Inorganic Compound preparation (Class XII). Any one.

practicals.title: Preparation of Potassium Ferric Oxalate
Preparation of Organic Compounds: Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, Aniline yellow/2-Naphthol dye
practicals.details: Organic Compound preparation (Class XII). Any one.

practicals.title: Tests for functional groups: Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic, amino
Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples/foodstuffs
Determination of molarity of KMnO4 solution by titration against Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate
practicals.details: Quantitative analysis (Class XII). Mandatory Section B.

practicals.title: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (Class XII)
practicals.details: Qualitative Analysis. Cations: NH4+. Anions: CO3 2-, S 2-, SO3 2-, Cl -, CH3COO -. Insoluble salts excluded.

practicals.title: Identification/Familiarity with apparatus
practicals.details: Visually Challenged Students Assessment. Identification of Beaker, tripod stand, wire gauze, glass rod, funnel, filter paper, Bunsen burner, test tube, etc.

practicals.title: Odor detection in qualitative analysis
practicals.details: Visually Challenged Students Assessment. Hands-on skill.

projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study of the methods of purification of water
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride, etc., depending upon the regional variation in drinking water
projects.details: Study of causes of presence of these ions above permissible limit.

projects.title: Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and the effect of addition of Sodium carbonate on it
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XI Practical: Total Marks - 30, Time - 2 Hours. Volumetric Analysis (08), Salt Analysis (08), Content Based Experiment (06), Project Work (04), Class record and viva (04)
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
Class XII Practical: Total Marks - 30. Volumetric Analysis (08), Salt Analysis (08), Content Based Experiment (06), Project Work (04), Class record and viva (04)
Visually Challenged Students Assessment: Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (5), Written test (10), Practical Record (5), Viva (10). Total - 30
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.
bloom_levels: Domain 1 - Remembering and Understanding: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Domain 2 - Applying: Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Domain 3 - Analysing, Evaluating and Creating: Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.
internal_assessment: Internal choices: 33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.
Written test for visually challenged students: Conducted at the time of practical examination, duration 30 minutes. Contains 15 skill-based very short answer questions; student answers any 10.
practical_assessment: Class XI Practical Syllabus Sections: Basic Laboratory Techniques (Cutting glass tube/rod, Bending, Drawing jet, Boring cork), Characterization and Purification (Melting point, Boiling point, Crystallization of Alum/Copper Sulphate/Benzoic Acid), Experiments based on pH (Determination of pH, Comparing strong/weak acids, Titration study, Common-ion effect), Chemical Equilibrium (Shift in equilibrium studies), Quantitative Estimation (Balance usage, Standard solution preparation, Titrations against Oxalic acid/Sodium carbonate), Qualitative Analysis (Anion/Cation detection, Detection of N/S/Cl in organics).

Class XII Practical Syllabus Sections: Surface Chemistry (Preparation of sols, Dialysis, Emulsifying agents), Chemical Kinetics (Effect of concentration/temp on rate, Reaction rates studies), Thermochemistry (Enthalpy of dissolution/neutralization/hydrogen bond formation), Electrochemistry (Variation of cell potential), Chromatography (Separation of pigments/inorganic mixtures), Preparation of Inorganic Compounds (Double salts/Potassium Ferric Oxalate), Preparation of Organic Compounds (Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, Aniline yellow/2-Naphthol dye), Tests for functional groups (Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic, amino), Characteristic tests of carbohydrates/fats/proteins, Determination of KMnO4 concentration.

Visually Challenged Practical Assessment: Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (5 marks), Odor detection in qualitative analysis. Written test based on prescribed practicals (10 marks). Practical Record maintenance (minimum 5 experiments) (5 marks). Viva questions covering basic theory, principle/concept, apparatus/materials/chemicals required, procedure, precautions, sources of error (10 marks).
project_assessment: Class XI Project Work: Marks - 4. Suggested Projects include checking bacterial contamination in drinking water, purification methods testing, hardness/Iron/Fluoride/Chloride presence study, foaming capacity of soaps, acidity of tea leaves, rate of evaporation, effect of acids/bases on fiber tensile strength, acidity of fruit and vegetable juices.

Class XII Project Work: Marks - 4. Suggested Projects include oxalate ions in guava, casein quantity in milk, soybean milk preparation/curd formation study, Potassium Bisulphate preservative effect, starch digestion by salivary amylase (pH/temp effects), fermentation rate comparison of wheat flour/gram flour/potato juice/carrot juice, extraction of essential oils from Saunf/Ajwain/Illaichi, common food adulterants study.
Project Guidelines: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information. Any other investigatory project involving about 10 periods can be chosen with teacher approval.

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XI Practical: Total Marks - 30, Time - 2 Hours. Volumetric Analysis (08), Salt Analysis (08), Content Based Experiment (06), Project Work (04), Class record and viva (04)
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
Class XII Practical: Total Marks - 30. Volumetric Analysis (08), Salt Analysis (08), Content Based Experiment (06), Project Work (04), Class record and viva (04)
Visually Challenged Students Assessment: Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (5), Written test (10), Practical Record (5), Viva (10). Total - 30
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.
bloom_levels: Domain 1 - Remembering and Understanding: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Domain 2 - Applying: Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Domain 3 - Analysing, Evaluating and Creating: Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.
internal_assessment: Internal choices: 33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.
Written test for visually challenged students: Conducted at the time of practical examination, duration 30 minutes. Contains 15 skill-based very short answer questions; student answers any 10.
practical_assessment: Class XI Practical Syllabus Sections: Basic Laboratory Techniques (Cutting glass tube/rod, Bending, Drawing jet, Boring cork), Characterization and Purification (Melting point, Boiling point, Crystallization of Alum/Copper Sulphate/Benzoic Acid), Experiments based on pH (Determination of pH, Comparing strong/weak acids, Titration study, Common-ion effect), Chemical Equilibrium (Shift in equilibrium studies), Quantitative Estimation (Balance usage, Standard solution preparation, Titrations against Oxalic acid/Sodium carbonate), Qualitative Analysis (Anion/Cation detection, Detection of N/S/Cl in organics).

Class XII Practical Syllabus Sections: Surface Chemistry (Preparation of sols, Dialysis, Emulsifying agents), Chemical Kinetics (Effect of concentration/temp on rate, Reaction rates studies), Thermochemistry (Enthalpy of dissolution/neutralization/hydrogen bond formation), Electrochemistry (Variation of cell potential), Chromatography (Separation of pigments/inorganic mixtures), Preparation of Inorganic Compounds (Double salts/Potassium Ferric Oxalate), Preparation of Organic Compounds (Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, Aniline yellow/2-Naphthol dye), Tests for functional groups (Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic, amino), Characteristic tests of carbohydrates/fats/proteins, Determination of KMnO4 concentration.

Visually Challenged Practical Assessment: Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (5 marks), Odor detection in qualitative analysis. Written test based on prescribed practicals (10 marks). Practical Record maintenance (minimum 5 experiments) (5 marks). Viva questions covering basic theory, principle/concept, apparatus/materials/chemicals required, procedure, precautions, sources of error (10 marks).
project_assessment: Class XI Project Work: Marks - 4. Suggested Projects include checking bacterial contamination in drinking water, purification methods testing, hardness/Iron/Fluoride/Chloride presence study, foaming capacity of soaps, acidity of tea leaves, rate of evaporation, effect of acids/bases on fiber tensile strength, acidity of fruit and vegetable juices.

Class XII Project Work: Marks - 4. Suggested Projects include oxalate ions in guava, casein quantity in milk, soybean milk preparation/curd formation study, Potassium Bisulphate preservative effect, starch digestion by salivary amylase (pH/temp effects), fermentation rate comparison of wheat flour/gram flour/potato juice/carrot juice, extraction of essential oils from Saunf/Ajwain/Illaichi, common food adulterants study.
Project Guidelines: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information. Any other investigatory project involving about 10 periods can be chosen with teacher approval.

long_chapter_names_reclassified: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques -> Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Conceptual Foundations and Measurement
competency_groups.description: Equip learners with tools to understand the working of Chemistry, develop conceptual foundations, represent phenomena at macroscopic/molecular/symbolic levels, identify patterns, and apply knowledge to real-life situations.
competencies: Understand nature of matter, properties, measurement, uncertainty, laws of chemical combination, Dalton's atomic theory
Calculate atomic/molecular masses, mole concept, molar mass, percentage composition, stoichiometry

competency_groups.title: Atomic Structure and Quantum Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to comprehend the evolution from sub-atomic particles discovery to quantum mechanical models, identifying patterns in atomic structure.
competencies: Trace discoveries of sub-atomic particles and development leading to Bohr's model

competency_groups.title: Periodicity and Classification
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify elements, understand periodic laws, electronic configurations, block types (s,p,d,f), and trends in properties.
competencies: Apply modern periodic law and interpret the present form of Periodic Table
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding Theories
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to represent chemical phenomena at molecular levels using various bonding theories (Kossel-Lewis, VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital).
competencies: Apply Kossel-Lewis approach and understand Ionic/Electrovalent bonds

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics and Spontaneity
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to analyze thermodynamic terms, enthalpy changes, calorimetry, spontaneity, Gibbs energy change, and equilibrium conditions.
competencies: Analyze applications of thermodynamics and measurement of ∆U and ∆H

competency_groups.title: Chemical Equilibrium Principles
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify patterns in dynamic equilibrium, apply laws of chemical equilibrium, and understand factors affecting equilibria including ionic equilibrium.
competencies: Apply Law of Chemical Equilibrium and interpret relationship between K, Q, and Gibbs Energy

competency_groups.title: Redox Reactions and Electrode Processes
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify oxidation-reduction reactions in terms of electron transfer, assign oxidation numbers, and understand electrode processes.
competencies: Classify redox reactions based on classical ideas and electron transfer mechanisms

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Fundamentals
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to represent organic compounds structurally, classify them, apply nomenclature rules (IUPAC), understand isomerism and reaction mechanisms.
competencies: Apply structural representations and classification of organic compounds
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbon Classification and Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify hydrocarbons, understand properties of alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, aromatic compounds, and assess carcinogenic/toxicity issues.
competencies: Differentiate between types of hydrocarbons based on structure and bonding

competency_groups.title: Solution Chemistry and Colligative Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to express concentration, understand solubility, vapor pressure, ideal/non-ideal solutions, and determine molar mass via colligative properties.
competencies: Apply concepts of solution types and expression of concentration

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry Applications
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand electrochemical cells, galvanic cells, Nernst equation, conductance, electrolytic processes, batteries, and corrosion.
competencies: Analyze functioning of Galvanic Cells and Electrolytic Cells

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics Analysis
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to analyze factors influencing reaction rates, apply integrated rate equations, and understand temperature dependence via collision theory.
competencies: Interpret rate of chemical reactions and factors influencing them

competency_groups.title: d-and f-Block Elements Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand position in periodic table, electronic configurations of transition elements and inner transition elements (Lanthanoids/Actinoids), and their applications.
competencies: Correlate general properties of Transition Elements with d-block configuration

competency_groups.title: Coordination Chemistry and Bonding
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to apply Werner's theory, understand nomenclature, isomerism, bonding in coordination compounds including metal carbonyls.
competencies: Apply definitions of terms pertaining to Coordination Compounds

competency_groups.title: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes Reactivity
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify, name, understand C-X bond nature, preparation methods, physical properties, and chemical reactions of halo compounds.
competencies: Analyze classification and nomenclature of Haloalkanes

competency_groups.title: Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify compounds, understand functional group structures, nomenclature, preparation of alcohols/phenols, ethers.
competencies: Differentiate between Alcohols, Phenols, and Ethers based on structure

competency_groups.title: Carbonyl Compounds Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand nomenclature of carbonyl groups, preparation methods (aldehydes/ketones/carboxylic acids), physical properties and chemical reactions.
competencies: Apply knowledge of Carbonyl Group structure and reactivity

competency_groups.title: Amines Synthesis and Reactivity
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand structure, classification, nomenclature of amines, preparation methods including Diazonium salts synthesis.
competencies: Analyze chemical reactions involving Amines

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules and Biochemistry Interface
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify patterns in carbohydrates, proteins, enzymes, vitamins, nucleic acids, hormones, linking chemistry with biology.
competencies: Classify Biomolecules into Carbohydrates, Proteins, Enzymes, Vitamins
